_meta.description: Full quadratic interaction network of the shipped 4-flag stack vs the millennial swing: per-pair forcing/gain/response table (eigenmode T0=3,900, Q=8), direct correlation of recovered excitation f(t) (inversion T0=3,850, Q=2) with total F² and each single-pair product, and F² extrema timing.
_meta.delta_n_locking: 726/760 and 1885/1919 pairs split by Δn=34 = 78,898-yr phase lock

verdict: ALL FOUR cycles do participate — but cooperatively, not democratically. The dominant structure is the ~3.6-kyr BAND: bond−hallstatt (3,695 yr) phase-locked with jose4−jose5 (3,530 yr) via the Δn = 34 splitting (8H/34 = 78,898-yr lock) — together 93% of the eigenmode-weighted response, and it takes all four flags to build it. The remaining pairs are suppressed by the resonator gain (hallstatt×jose4: 0.2% of response — its earlier "driver" role was in-window envelope-extremum TIMING, not power). Driving-side: uniform total F² correlates with the recovered excitation only at r = +0.22, while bond×hallstatt only reaches r = -0.68 — the coupling is selective (pair-dependent efficiency/sign), though with ~2.7 kyr of smoothed record the effective dof are few (~6) and no single-pair attribution is statistically decisive. Note the recovered FORCING correlates with products that contain sum tones (e.g. bond×hall 914 yr) while the RESPONSE contains none — exactly the resonator low-pass signature (|H| at 914 yr ≈ 0.06).
